answer:
### Mentions of "Wine" (Literal and Metaphorical)

Wine (and compounds like "wine-cup", "wine-hall") appears frequently, especially as a Sufi symbol of spiritual ecstasy in Urdu/Persian poetry (Iqbal, Ghalib, Zauq), but also literally in Old English poetry (*Andreas*), Dharmashastras (e.g., rice-wine), and economic discussions (wine trade/production). Below are key works with examples; full searches yielded ~200+ hits (many poetic repetitions).

#### Cynewulf, *Andreas* (Ch. 1, Translation)
- Jesus turns water to wine as a miracle.[^1]
- "Wine-halls wasted" during famine.[^2]
- "Wine-city" (Miletus) as a mead-hall metaphor.[^3]
- Andreas instructed to dwell in the "wine-city".[^4]

#### Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara* (Multiple Parts/Chapters)
Wine symbolizes love, divine intoxication, unity. ~50 hits across poems.
- Part 1, Ch. 4: Heart ready to break holds wine.[^5]
- Part 1, Ch. 5: Votary of wine blooms like a garden.[^6]
- Part 1, Ch. 9: Abandon wine-drinking even if lawful.[^7]
- Part 1, Ch. 17: Hearts drunk on mind's wines.[^8]
- Part 2, Ch. 3: Joy in wine trade.[^9]
- Part 3, Ch. 4: New drinkers, new wine.[^10]
- Part 3, Ch. 7: Drunk on idle pleasure's wine.[^11]
- Part 3, Ch. 20: Carry wine of truth; Persian flask, Hijazi wine.[^12]
- Others: Chs. 11, 14, 18, 23, 25, 28, 32, 36, 40, 44 (Part 1); Chs. 4, 8, 11, 14, 26, 28 (Part 2); Chs. 11, 14, 17, 20, 25 (Part 3).

#### Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib* (Multiple Ghazals)
~150+ hits; wine as love/ecstasy metaphor ubiquitous.
- Ch. 1: Trade heaven for wine; oath to quit wine.[^13]
- Ch. 2: Saqi's pride cures thirst; pulse of wine.[^14]
- Ch. 6: Fire of wine burns market.[^15]
- Ch. 7: Drunken eyes waste feast.[^16]
- Ch. 14: Worldly sorrows, scant wine?[^17]
- Chs. 20, 22, 23, 29, 35, 43, 45, 48–50, 54, 59, 61–62, 66, 68, 70, 75, 77–78, 83, 85, 100, 105–106, 108, 111, 116–117, 128–132, 136–139, 141, 145–146, 150–151, 155–156, 160–161, 165, 167, 169, 171–172, 174, 176–177, 181, 186, 190–191, 193, 195, 198, 202, 204–205, 208, 213–214, 219, 231, 234, 239 (all mention wine/cups/flasks).

#### Sheikh Ibrahim Zauq, *Diwan-E-Zauq* (Multiple)
~20 hits.
- Ch. 8: Pouring wine.[^18]
- Ch. 11: Flask's gurgling song.[^19]
- Ch. 13: Drinking wine prayer.[^20]
- Ch. 15: Water to wine; jars of wine.[^21]
- Chs. 25, 29, 37–38, 41, 45, 50, 52, 54, 58, 60.

#### Other Works
- *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana* (Ch. 1): Hall with rice-wine.[^22]
- *Manusmṛti* (Ch. 6): No sin in wine (natural inclination).[^23]
- Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie* (Vols. I/II): Wine-growing, merchants, Muhammad banning wine.[^24]
- Charles Comte, *Traité de législation* (Vol. II): Wine libations.

### Mentions of "Intoxication" (Literal/Metaphorical/Legal)
Fewer (~50 hits); often legal (incapacity in Smṛtis) or poetic (spiritual).

#### Dharmashastras/Smṛtis
Legal contexts: Intoxicated persons lack capacity for contracts/testimony.
- *Manusmṛti* (Ch. 7): Intoxication as vice.[^25]
- *Kunjarakarna* (Ch. 1): Doubt and intoxication break understanding.[^26]
- *Pārśara Smṛti* (Ch. 10): Woman overcome by intoxication.[^27]
- *Ślokāntara* (Ch. 1): Causes of intoxication (palm wine, etc.).[^28]
- *Vṛttisaṃsana* (Ch. 1): Chain from desire to intoxication/violence.[^29]
- Others: *Bṛhaspati Smṛti*, *Kātyāyana Smṛti*, *Nārada Smṛti*, *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, *Yājñavalkya Smṛti* (intoxicated/insane invalid deeds/witnesses).

#### Poetry
- Iqbal/Ghalib/Zauq: Spiritual "intoxication" (e.g., *Bang-E-Dara* Ch. 17: Drunk on mind's wines).

### Mentions of "Drunk/Drunken" (~170 hits)
Overlaps heavily with above; poetic (drunk on love/Fate/wine), legal (drunkards as unfit), narrative (drunken fools).

- *Andreas* (Ch. 1): Heathens drunk on blood.[^30]
- Iqbal: Stars drunk on Fate (*Bang-E-Dara* Part 2, Ch. 14); hearts drunk (*Part 1*, Ch. 17).[^31]
- Ghalib/Zauq: Drunken eyes/glances, friends.[^32]
- Smṛtis: Drunkards unfit witnesses (*Kātyāyana* Ch. 33); drunken elephant (*Ślokāntara*).
- Economic: National drunkenness/climate (*Comte/Dunoyer*).

The corpus (37 works) emphasizes wine's symbolic role in mysticism over literal consumption. Legal texts treat intoxication as impairing agency. No exhaustive verbatim list possible (200+ unique paras), but all via search_corpus("wine|drunk|intoxicat*").

[^8]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, Ch. 17, [p-6ebcbf](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/17-dard-e-ishq/translation/#p-6ebcbf).
